corpus.title: World Cup 2026: Spain return to Madrid in triumph
corpus.signal_text:
World Cup 2026: Spain return to Madrid in triumph

Spain reunited with their supporters in Madrid less than 24 hours after winning the World Cup final against Argentina (1-0, after extra time). The players of La Roja, alongside head coach Luis de la Fuente, presented the trophy upon their arrival at the airport before taking part in celebrations across the capital. More than 1 million people attended the parade, which ended with a major concert at Cibeles Square.

corpus.title: 2026 World Cup: La Roja celebrates title with victory parade in Madrid

2026 World Cup: La Roja celebrates title with victory parade in Madrid

More than one million people took to the streets of Madrid to welcome La Roja after their World Cup triumph. 🇪🇸

corpus.title: Coupe du monde 2026 : l’Espagne célèbre son sacre mondial à Madrid
corpus.signal_text:
Coupe du monde 2026 : l’Espagne célèbre son sacre mondial à Madrid

L’Espagne a retrouvé ses supporters, à Madrid, moins de 24 heures après son titre mondial remporté face à l’Argentine (1-0, a.p.). Les joueurs de la Roja, accompagnés de leur sélectionneur Luis de la Fuente, ont présenté le trophée dès leur arrivée à l'aéroport avant de participer aux célébrations dans les rues de la capitale. Plus d’un million de personnes ont assisté à la parade, conclue sur la place de Cybèle par un grand concert.

corpus.title: Torres fires Spain past Argentina in extra time to win World Cup, denying Messi a 2nd title
corpus.signal_text:
Torres fires Spain past Argentina in extra time to win World Cup, denying Messi a 2nd title

Ferran Torres' first goal of this year's World Cup was the goal of his life, and it put Spain back atop the soccer world. Denying Lionel Messi a second straight title, Torres came off the bench and scored in the 106th minute and Spain ended Argentina's reign with a 1-0 win on Sunday.

corpus.title: 5 questions that continue to linger after the FIFA World Cup
corpus.signal_text:
5 questions that continue to linger after the FIFA World Cup

The curtain has fallen on the 2026 FIFA World Cup, the grandest and most expansive edition yet, hosted across the United States, Canada and Mexico. Spain emerged triumphant in a tense final against Argentina, etching their name on the trophy in a display of technical brilliance. Yet as the echoes of the stadiums fade, the tournament departs with more than memories of footballing excellence. It leaves behind a trail of controversies and unanswered questions that may linger long after the final wh

corpus.title: Spain wins second World Cup after beating Argentina in final
corpus.signal_text:
Spain wins second World Cup after beating Argentina in final

After a 16-year wait, Spain has claimed a second men's World Cup. Ferran Torres scored the winner in a deserved 1-0 victory over Argentina, which means Spain is now men's and women's world champions.

corpus.title: Spain's World Cup glory denies Trump and Bibi their dream finale
corpus.signal_text:
Spain's World Cup glory denies Trump and Bibi their dream finale

TEHRAN – The curtain fell on the 2026 FIFA World Cup in the United States with an ending that few in Washington had hoped to witness. Instead of Argentina lifting the trophy at MetLife Stadium in New Jersey, Spain emerged victorious after defeating the defending champions 1-0 in extra time, turning what many expected to be a political celebration into a night of disappointment for President Donald Trump and his closest international allies.

corpus.title: Palestinians celebrate Spain’s World Cup victory
corpus.signal_text:
Palestinians celebrate Spain’s World Cup victory

Hundreds of Palestinians across Gaza and the Occupied West Bank celebrated Spain’s World Cup victory, gathering around makeshift screens, cheering in stadiums and taking to the streets in celebration of Spain’s victory and the support its government has given to Palestine.

corpus.title: Gaza celebrates World Cup victory of Spanish team they took into their hearts
corpus.signal_text:
Gaza celebrates World Cup victory of Spanish team they took into their hearts

People gather at cafes and outdoor screenings to support team after Spain’s backing for Palestinian rights The Spanish football team’s World Cup win has been celebrated in the streets of Gaza, where Spain’s support for Palestinian rights and Israel’s backing for the Argentinian team turned the competition into more than a football showdown.Gathered at cafes and outdoor screenings, Palestinians defied the risk of Israeli attacks to cheer on a team they had taken to their hearts.
